meta.title: Mercedes-Benz M116/M117/M119 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost

hero.h1: Mercedes-Benz M116/M117/M119 Engine — Specs, Reliability & Replacement Cost
hero.subHeadline: The M116, M117 and M119 are Mercedes' classic V8 petrol engines — legendary smoothness and durability, but chain tensioners and distributor caps need regular attention. Backed by real UK enquiries [ESTIMATED].

rows.value: Confidence: 90% confidence — based on decades of owner feedback, specialist workshop consensus, and extensive forum data; proprietary enquiry figures are estimated.
Reconditioned supply midpoint ~£4,000 → falls in £3,000–£4,999 band

rows.metric: Most mechanical work straightforward; chain tensioners and timing components require specialist attention

rows.value: Good aftermarket support for common items; some M119-specific parts getting scarce

rows.metric: Depends on host vehicle value — classic/collector values may justify; everyday examples may approach scrap threshold

rows.value: Sum of the five rows above

rows.metric: One-line verdict: The M116/M117/M119 are among Mercedes' finest V8s — smooth, powerful and durable — but age-related chain tensioner issues affect all examples — inspect timing components and injection systems carefully.
rows.value: Best For: Classic Mercedes enthusiasts, collectors, and anyone seeking analogue V8 refinement with legendary build quality.

compatibility.crossBrandNote: Cross-link: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand fitment.

costGuide.labourEstimate: Fitting an M116/M117/M119 typically takes 12–18 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [ESTIMATED]. Timing chain and tensioner work is a specialist job.
costGuide.sharedCostNote: Exclusive to Mercedes-Benz — no cross-brand cost comparison applies.

cta.label: → Compare M116/M117/M119 engine prices from UK specialists

items.question: How much does an M116/M117/M119 engine replacement cost?
items.answer: A reconditioned M116/M117/M119 typically costs £3,000–£5,000 supply only [ESTIMATED], varying by displacement and specific model (M119 5.0L generally the most expensive). Fitting adds £800–£1,800 depending on labour rates.

items.question: Is the M116/M117/M119 a reliable engine?
items.answer: The M116/M117/M119 are widely regarded as some of the most reliable V8s ever built, earning 4.5★ out of 5 [ESTIMATED] — the M119 in particular is legendary for durability, with many examples exceeding 200,000 miles. Regular chain tensioner maintenance is the key to longevity.

items.question: What is the most common M116/M117/M119 failure?
items.answer: Timing chain tensioner wear is the most common issue — slack chains can cause valve timing issues or catastrophic failure if neglected. Leaking distributor caps and worn injection components are also common on higher‑mileage examples. Tensioner replacement typically needed between 80,000–120,000 miles.

items.question: Which Mercedes models use the M116/M117/M119?
items.answer: The M116 (3.5L) and M117 (4.5–5.6L) powered the W116/W126 S‑Class, R107 SL, and C126 SEC. The M119 (4.2–5.0L) powered the W140 S‑Class, R129 SL, W124 E‑Class (500E), and later W463 G‑Class.

items.question: How long does the M116/M117/M119 last?
items.answer: With proper maintenance, 200,000–300,000 miles is common, and well‑maintained M119s have exceeded 400,000 miles. The engines are overbuilt; body rust and electrical issues usually claim the car first. Regular timing chain and tensioner inspection is essential.
